user_message: How should I coach Marcus Williams on Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 09:06 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 17:50 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery security posture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 16:59 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:14:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - security posture

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
